dimensions_mm: unknown (full system variant: 180 x 183 x 211 mm)
compatible_drones: Small UAVs and tactical platforms; integrated with DeltaQuad, Auterion Skynode-based systems, PX4/QGroundControl platforms
price_source: Not publicly listed; quote-based pricing only. Contact nextvision-sys.com or authorized resellers (Height Technologies, Harris Aerial, BuckEye Cam).
availability: Available through manufacturer and authorized resellers
notable_features: Three EO lenses for flexible operation
Wide-area scanning HFOV 60 degrees to 0.75 degree DFOV
MicroSD card recording with GPS metadata
near_miss_reason: Exceeds 10x optical zoom (40x) and is well under 1000g (145g), but price is not publicly available and likely exceeds $10,000 given its defense/military market positioning. Cannot confirm price criteria.
research_notes: NextVision is an Israeli defense company. The X80 is marketed to military and security customers with quote-based pricing. The 145g weight refers to the camera head; a full gimbal system variant weighs approximately 1.5kg. Stabilization range data sourced from Height Technologies product page. The X80 connects via Ethernet with MAVLink protocol per Auterion/PX4 integration documentation.
